# mlir-opt crashes with UNREACHABLE in linalg.elementwise when operands have vector types

**Author:** compilerStudyer
**URL:** https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/178363
**Status:** Open
**Labels:** mlir:linalg, crash, generated by fuzzer

## Body

mlir-opt crashes with an UNREACHABLE when parsing a linalg.elementwise operation whose operands are vector types instead of tensors or memrefs.

The crash happens during parsing / region construction, before any pass pipeline is executed.

```mlir
module {
  func.func @main() {
    %0 = spirv.Constant dense<0> : vector<4xi32>
    %1 = gpu.grid_dim  x
    %2:4 = vector.to_elements %0 : vector<4xi32>
    %3 = vector.multi_reduction <add>, %0, %2#0 [0] : vector<4xi32> to i32
    %4 = vector.reduction <add>, %0, %3 : vector<4xi32> into i32
    %5 = linalg.elementwise kind=#linalg.elementwise_kind<add> ins(%0, %0 : vector<4xi32>, vector<4xi32>) outs(%0 : vector<4xi32>) -> tensor<4xi32>
    return
  }
}
```
```
mlir-opt test.mlir
```
```
unsupported non numeric type
UNREACHABLE executed at mlir/lib/Dialect/Linalg/IR/LinalgOps.cpp:514!
PLEASE submit a bug report to https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/ and include the crash backtrace and instructions to reproduce the bug.
Stack dump:
0.      Program arguments: mlir-opt test.mlir
1.      MLIR Parser: custom op parser 'builtin.module'
2.      MLIR Parser: custom op parser 'func.func'

 #3 0x0000796393245330 (/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6+0x45330)

 #5 0x000079639329eb2c __pthread_kill_internal ./nptl/pthread_kill.c:78:10
```

```mlir
    %0 = spirv.Constant dense&lt;0&gt; : vector&lt;4xi32&gt;
    %1 = gpu.grid_dim  x

    return
  }
}
```

---

### Comment 2 - IamYJLee

Is this an issue that should be handled by the verifier or by an explicit condition check?
It seems that this case should be diagnosed earlier, rather than reaching an llvm_unreachable.
I’d like to try fixing this issue.

---

### Comment 3 - matthias-springer

This happens during parsing. It looks like `emitError` is not passed when calling `RegionBuilderHelper::buildBinaryFn`. (See call site in `ElementwiseOp::regionBuilder`.)

---

@javedabsar1

### Comment 5 - IamYJLee

> This happens during parsing. It looks like `emitError` is not passed when calling `RegionBuilderHelper::buildBinaryFn`. (See call site in `ElementwiseOp::regionBuilder`.)

Thanks for the pointer!

### Comment 6 - javedabsar1

This is interesting. I never imagined vectors being passed to linalg.elementwise.  The elementwise op must lower to linalg.generic which only accepts memref/tensors.
Probably we need a clean 'check memref or tensor' and bail out nicely on 'linalg.elementwise ... vector<4xi32>, vector<4xi32>) '

---

### Comment 7 - IamYJLee

> This is interesting. I never imagined vectors being passed to linalg.elementwise. The elementwise op must lower to linalg.generic which only accepts memref/tensors. Probably we need a clean 'check memref or tensor' and bail out nicely on 'linalg.elementwise ... vector<4xi32>, vector<4xi32>) '

Just to make sure I’m on the right track, is this the expected outcome for the issue I’m working on?
```bash
test.mlir:8:64: error: custom op 'linalg.elementwise' Cannot build binary Linalg operation: expects allComplex, allFloatingPoint, or allInteger, got 'vector<4xi32>' and 'vector<4xi32>'
    %5 = linalg.elementwise kind=#linalg.elementwise_kind<add> ins(%0, %0 : vector<4xi32>, vector<4xi32>) outs(%0 : vector<4xi32>) -> tensor<4xi32>
                                                               ^
test.mlir:9:5: error: custom op 'linalg.elementwise' unable to parse elemwise op
    return
    ^
```

I still don’t fully understand how the elementwise op should be lowered to linalg.generic.

---

### Comment 8 - rengolin

The types must match. `tensor -> tensor`, `memref -> memref` and `vector -> vector`

---
